words.definition: a place that protects you from weather
an open space in the woods with no trees
making a low rumbling sound (like a hungry stomach)
a squeaking sound that old doors make
a large, special meal with lots of food
what someone is really like inside
to get away from a dangerous place

questions.question: What happened to Hansel's trail of breadcrumbs?
options: They got wet in the rain

questions.question: What did the children smell in the woods?
What was the house made of?
Why were the children's stomachs growling?
What did the old woman look like?
What did the old woman really want to do?
options: Help the children find their way home
She had a terrible plan for them

questions.question: What did the witch do to Hansel?
options: She locked him in a cage

questions.question: What did the witch want Gretel to do?
How did Hansel trick the witch?
options: He held out a chicken bone instead of his finger

questions.question: How did Gretel defeat the witch?
options: She pushed her into the oven
She locked her in the cage

informationalTexts.title: Fire Trucks
informationalTexts.content:
1. a) hoses, ladders, and axes
2. b) to warn other drivers to get out of the way

informationalTexts.title: Penguins
informationalTexts.content:
1. b) in cold places near the ocean
2. a) fish, squid, and krill

informationalTexts.title: The Sun
informationalTexts.content:
1. b) a star
2. a) it gives Earth light and heat that plants and animals need

informationalTexts.title: Librarians
informationalTexts.content:
1. a) to help people find books and information
2. a) read stories and plan programs

informationalTexts.title: Dentists
informationalTexts.content:
1. a) little holes in teeth caused by bacteria
2. b) twice a year

grammarLessons.content:
**Nouns**

This lesson focuses on Nouns. As you read today's story, pay attention to examples of this concept.

**Key Points:**
• Understand the concept
• Find examples in the story
• Practice using it correctly

**Practice:**
Apply what you learned by completing the exercises.

**Remember:** Nouns is an important language skill!
grammarLessons.objective: Students will understand and apply Nouns.

languageLessons.content:
**ABC Order**

This language lesson teaches ABC Order. Look for examples in today's story.

**Key Ideas:**
• Learn the concept
• Find examples
• Practice the skill

**Practice Activities:**
Complete the exercises to master this skill.

**Remember:** Practice makes perfect!
languageLessons.objective: Students will learn about ABC Order.

languageLessons.content:
Rhyming words sound the SAME at the end. They make poems and stories fun to read aloud!

**Rhyming Words:**
• cat, hat, mat, sat (all end with -at)
• play, day, say, way (all end with -ay)
• book, look, cook, took (all end with -ook)

**From today's story:**
Listen for words that rhyme as you read!

**Practice - Find the rhyme:**
1. Which word rhymes with "dog"? (log, big, cat)
2. Which word rhymes with "jump"? (run, bump, sit)
3. Which word rhymes with "bed"? (chair, red, sleep)

**Remember:** Listen to the END of words to find rhymes!
languageLessons.objective: Students will identify and create rhyming words.
